<Profile>### IDENTITY

- **Age:** 35  
- **Gender:** Male  
- **Experience level:** **Advanced** — 7 years consistent training; 5 NPC shows (2022–2024), best placing **2nd at a regional**; currently self-coaching prep.

---

### GOALS
- **Primary goal(s):** **Muscle building with competitive bodybuilding prep focus (NPC Men’s Physique)**
  - Bring up: **rear delts, lateral delts, triceps long head**
  - **Stage weight target:** **175 lb**
  - **Current weight:** **185 lb** *(as reported; logged 2026-02-27 pending confirmation of measurement date)*
  - **Nutrition context:** in a **caloric deficit (~2200 kcal/day)** *(as reported; logged 2026-02-27)*

---

### TRAINING CONTEXT

#### Schedule & Availability
- **Available training days:** **5 days/week** (desired frequency = 5)

  - Uses **knee sleeves** on leg days

#### Preferences
- Training style: **controlled tempo**, strong **mind-muscle connection**
- Volume target: **high volume ~15–20 sets/muscle/week**

- Coaching: **self-coached** for current prep

### METRICS
*(Ordered toward bodybuilding/body comp priority; strength numbers not provided yet.)*

**Body Composition**
- **Body weight:** **185 lb** *(as of 2026-02-27, user-reported; measurement date not specified)*
- **Stage weight target:** **175 lb** *(goal; event-tied)*

### LOG

- Noted specialization goals: **rear delts, lateral delts, triceps long head**

If you answer these, I can tighten the profile for program design:  
1) Confirm the **actual event date/year** (is it July 19, **2026**?) and what week of prep you’re currently in.  
2) Provide typical **session length** and preferred training time windows.  
3) Share 3–6 key performance anchors (e.g., hack squat load x reps, leg press load x reps, cable lateral raise stack x reps, incline press, pull-up/pulldown numbers).</Profile>

<Plan>## Header
- **Program name:** Men’s Physique Specialization (Delts/Long-Head Triceps) + Knee-Smart Lower Body — 5-Day PPL–UL Hybrid  
- **Program Owner:** GymText  
- **User:** David Park  
- **Goal:** Muscle building with competitive bodybuilding prep focus (NPC Men’s Physique); bring up **rear delts, lateral delts, triceps long head** while maintaining lower body with knee constraints  
- **Duration:** **Ongoing with phase cycling** (contest-prep peak can replace Phase 3 once event date/year is confirmed)

---

## Program Philosophy
David is an advanced bodybuilder training 5 days/week in a commercial gym with strong equipment access and a clear specialization goal (rear delts, lateral delts, long-head triceps) while currently in a caloric deficit. This program uses a **PPL–UL hybrid** to keep performance high on compounds, distribute fatigue, and allow **higher weekly frequency** for delts/triceps without wrecking recovery. Lower body work is built around **knee-tolerant patterns** (hack squat, leg press, Smith, controlled split squat) while avoiding heavy back squats beyond his comfort threshold and avoiding deep lunge positions. Progression is **autoregulated (RPE/RIR) with double progression**, using strategic deloads every 4th week to preserve joints and performance—especially important in a deficit.

---

# Phase 1: Volume Base + Specialization Foundation (Weeks 1–4)

### Weekly Pattern
**Overall targets (weekly hard sets):**
- Lateral delts: **18–24**
- Rear delts: **16–22**
- Triceps (long-head emphasis): **14–18** (with 6–10 sets directly long-head biased)
- Chest/Back: **10–16** each (maintenance-to-slight gain in deficit)
- Quads/Hams/Glutes: **10–16** each (knee-smart execution)

> **Rep/effort guidelines (Phase 1):** Mostly **6–12 reps** on primary lifts and **10–20 reps** on isolations at **RPE 7–9** (leave ~1–3 reps in reserve on most sets).

---

## Day 1 — Push (Chest + Delts + Triceps)
- **Focus:** Pressing strength maintenance + delt/triceps volume
- **Volume:** ~22–28 working sets

1) **Incline DB Press**: 4 × 6–10 @ RPE 7–9  
2) **Machine Chest Press (neutral grip if available)**: 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 8  
3) **Cable Lateral Raise (single-arm, slight lean)**: 5 × 12–20 @ RPE 8–9  
   - *high-skill MMC movement for lateral delt cap; easy to progress without joint cost*  
4) **High-to-Low Cable Fly**: 2–3 × 12–15 @ RPE 8  
5) **Overhead Cable Triceps Extension (rope, split stance)**: 4 × 10–15 @ RPE 8–9  
   - *biases long head via shoulder flexion; cornerstone for your stated weak point*  
6) **Cable Pressdown (straight bar or rope)**: 3 × 10–15 @ RPE 8  
7) **Optional: Pec Deck**: 1–2 × 12–20 @ RPE 8 (only if chest recovery is strong)

---

## Day 2 — Pull (Back Width/Thickness + Rear Delts)
- **Focus:** Back density + high rear-delt stimulus
- **Volume:** ~20–26 working sets

1) **Weighted Pull-Up or Neutral-Grip Pulldown**: 4 × 6–10 @ RPE 7–9  
2) **Chest-Supported Row (machine or incline bench DB row)**: 4 × 8–12 @ RPE 8  
3) **One-Arm Cable Lat Pull-In (lat “sweep” line)**: 3 × 10–15 @ RPE 8  
4) **Reverse Pec Deck**: 4 × 12–20 @ RPE 8–9  
   - *rear delt staple with stable setup and high repeatability in a deficit*  
5) **Cable Rear Delt Fly (cross-body)**: 3 × 12–20 @ RPE 8–9  
6) **EZ-Bar or DB Curl**: 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 8  
7) **Cable Hammer Curl**: 2 × 10–15 @ RPE 8

---

## Day 3 — Legs (Knee-Smart Quad Bias + Hamstrings)
- **Focus:** Quad stimulus without heavy back squats; hamstring balance
- **Volume:** ~18–24 working sets

1) **Hack Squat (controlled depth, no forced deep ROM)**: 4 × 6–10 @ RPE 7–9  
   - *quad overload with stable pattern; avoids the back squat load/position you don’t tolerate*  
2) **Leg Press (feet mid-to-low for quads)**: 3 × 10–15 @ RPE 8  
3) **Seated Leg Curl**: 4 × 8–12 @ RPE 8–9  
4) **Smith Machine Romanian Deadlift**: 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 8  
5) **Leg Extension (2-sec squeeze at top)**: 2–3 × 12–20 @ RPE 9  
   - *high local quad stimulus with low systemic fatigue; useful in deficit*  
6) **Standing Calf Raise**: 4 × 8–12 @ RPE 8–9  
7) **Tibialis Raise (machine or DB)**: 2 × 15–25 @ RPE 8  
   - *supports knee/ankle robustness and lower-leg balance*

---

## Day 4 — Upper (Back/Chest Maintenance + Arms, Delt Touch)
- **Focus:** Upper density + arm volume; keep delts frequent
- **Volume:** ~20–26 working sets

1) **Machine Row (Hammer Strength if available)**: 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 8  
2) **Incline Smith Press or Machine Incline Press**: 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 8  
3) **Cable Lateral Raise (different angle than Day 1)**: 4 × 12–20 @ RPE 8–9  
4) **Pullover (cable or machine)**: 2–3 × 10–15 @ RPE 8  
5) **Overhead DB Triceps Extension (seated)**: 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 8  
   - *long-head bias with slightly heavier loading than cables for progression*  
6) **Rope Pressdown**: 2–3 × 12–15 @ RPE 8–9  
7) **Incline DB Curl**: 3 × 10–15 @ RPE 8  
8) **Optional: Face Pull (to forehead, external rotation)**: 2 × 15–20 @ RPE 7–8  
   - *scapular/up-rotator balance for shoulder health given high delt volume*

---

## Day 5 — Lower + Delts/Arms (Glute/Ham Bias + Specialization Pump)
- **Focus:** Posterior chain + extra delts/triceps without joint overload
- **Volume:** ~18–26 working sets

1) **Romanian Deadlift (DB or barbell)**: 3 × 6–10 @ RPE 7–9  
2) **Hip Thrust (Smith or machine)**: 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 8  
3) **Lying or Seated Leg Curl**: 3 × 10–15 @ RPE 8–9  
4) **Bulgarian Split Squat (controlled, avoid deep knee travel)**: 2–3 × 8–12/side @ RPE 8  
   - *single-leg stability and glute/quad stimulus while respecting knee tolerance; keep ROM “clean”*  
5) **Cable Lateral Raise (partials after full ROM)**: 3 × 15–25 @ RPE 9  
6) **Rear Delt Cable Fly or Reverse Pec Deck**: 3 × 12–20 @ RPE 8–9  
7) **Overhead Cable Triceps Extension**: 2–3 × 12–20 @ RPE 8–9

---

## Progression Strategy (Phase 1)
**Primary method: double progression + load micro-jumps**
- For each exercise with a rep range (e.g., 8–12):  
  - Keep load the same until you can hit the **top end of the range on all sets** at the target RPE.  
  - Then add load next week (typical: **+5 lb DBs**, **+5–10 lb machines**, or **+1 pin** on cables) and work back up.
- **Effort targets:**  
  - Compounds: last set usually **RPE 8–9** (0–2 RIR)  
  - Isolations: last set often **RPE 9** (0–1 RIR) with clean tempo and stable form
- **Deload (Week 4):**  
  - Reduce volume to **~50–60%** of normal sets, keep intensity moderate (**RPE 6–7**)  
  - Keep movement patterns the same; no grinders; emphasize joint feel and MMC
- **Transition to Phase 2:** after Week 4 deload, if performance is stable (no regressions across multiple key lifts) and joints feel good.

---

# Phase 2: Intensification + Strength-Backed Hypertrophy (Weeks 5–8)

### Weekly Pattern
Same 5-day structure, but:
- Slightly **lower total sets** for chest/back/legs (maintenance volume)
- Keep **delt/triceps specialization volume high**
- Add **heavier “anchor” sets** early in sessions (more 5–8 rep work) to preserve strength in a deficit

**Key changes (examples):**
- First compound on Days 1–3: include **1 top set** (5–8 reps @ RPE 8–9) + **2–3 back-off sets** (8–12 @ RPE 8)
- Keep isolations mostly **10–20 reps**, adding intensity techniques sparingly (1 technique per session max)

### Progression Strategy (Phase 2)
- **Top set progression:** add **+1 rep** week-to-week until top of range, then load jump.  
- **Back-off sets:** maintain volume quality; don’t chase load at the expense of MMC.  
- **Deload (Week 8):** same as Phase 1 (50–60% sets; RPE 6–7).  
- Transition when performance stalls for 2 straight weeks or fatigue/joint stress climbs.

---

# Phase 3: Contest-Cut Support / “Hold Size While Getting Leaner” (Weeks 9–12)

### Weekly Pattern
Same split, but overall goal shifts:
- Maintain fullness and detail with **slightly reduced systemic fatigue**
- Prioritize **execution, MMC, and recoverability**
- Legs remain knee-smart and slightly lower fatigue if cardio increases

**Typical adjustments:**
- Reduce total sets by ~10–20% (except delts/triceps can stay near the top end if recovery allows)
- Compounds mostly **6–10 reps @ RPE 7–8** (fewer grinders)
- Isolations stay **12–25 reps**, high quality

### Progression Strategy (Phase 3)
- Progression becomes **performance-maintenance biased**:
  - Aim to **match reps** at the same load/RPE week to week.
  - Small load increases only when recovery is clearly there.
- **Deload (Week 12):** optional depending on fatigue; if pushing hard dieting/cardio, treat Week 12 as a planned deload.

> If you confirm an actual show date, Phase 3 can be replaced by a true **peak block (8–12 weeks)** with more explicit fatigue management, posing practice integration, and a taper into show week.

---

## Phase Cycling
**Default cycle:**  
**Phase 1 (4w) → Phase 2 (4w) → Phase 3 (4w) → repeat**

**Triggers to move phases (not just time):**
- **Move earlier to Phase 2** if loads/reps are climbing smoothly and joints feel great.
- **Stay longer in Phase 1** if knee or elbow/shoulder irritation increases, or if deficit is aggressive and performance is sliding.
- **Move to Phase 3** when dieting stress/cardio volume increases and you need fatigue control to preserve muscle.

**Exercise rotation rule (every new Phase 1):**
- Keep the **movement pattern** but rotate 1–2 accessories per muscle group (e.g., swap reverse pec deck ↔ cable rear delt fly; swap machine press model; swap curl variations) to maintain stimulus and reduce overuse.

---

## Modification History
- **2026-02-27:** Initial program created based on profile (5-day PPL–UL hybrid; knee-smart lower body; specialization volume for lateral/rear delts + long-head triceps).

---

If you answer one item, I can “lock in” a true prep timeline: **Is the Western Regional date/year actually July 19, 2026 (and are you currently ~20 weeks out)?** Once confirmed, I’ll convert Phase 3 into a meet-style **peak/taper** for bodybuilding (volume and fatigue reductions mapped to the final 6–8 weeks).</Plan>

<Week># Microcycle — Week of 2026-02-23

**Program:** Men’s Physique Specialization (Delts/Long-Head Triceps) + Knee-Smart Lower Body — 5-Day PPL–UL Hybrid  
**Phase:** Phase 1 — Volume Base + Specialization Foundation (Week 1 of 4)  
**User:** David Park  

## Schedule
- **Mon (2/23):** Push — Chest + Delts + Triceps (LA Fitness)
- **Tue (2/24):** Pull — Back + Rear Delts (LA Fitness)
- **Wed (2/25):** Legs — Knee-Smart Quad Bias + Hamstrings (LA Fitness)
- **Thu (2/26):** Rest / steps + optional posing
- **Fri (2/27):** Upper — Maintenance + Arms + Delt Touch (LA Fitness)
- **Sat (2/28):** Lower + Delts/Arms — Posterior Chain + Specialization Pump (LA Fitness)
- **Sun (3/1):** Rest

## Week Overview
Phase 1 / Week 1 is about establishing **repeatable working loads** and nailing execution (controlled tempo, strong MMC) while you’re in a deficit. Expect most compounds to land around **RPE 7–8** and isolations to **RPE 8–9** without grinders. Lower body stays **knee-smart** (hack/press/Smith/BSS with controlled ROM; knee sleeves on leg days).

> **Load note (important):** Because no recent performance anchors were provided, the listed weights are **conservative “starter prescriptions.”** Keep the weight if it hits the target RPE; otherwise adjust **±5–15%** *within the session* to land the intended effort while maintaining perfect form.

---

# Workout — Monday, February 23, 2026
**Focus:** Pressing maintenance + delt/triceps volume (long-head emphasis)  
**Location:** LA Fitness  
**Duration:** ~90 minutes

## Warm-Up (10 minutes)
1. Bike or incline walk: **5 min** easy
2. Band shoulder series (light band):
   - Band pull-aparts: **2 × 15**
   - Band external rotations: **2 × 12/side**
3. Scap push-ups: **1 × 12**
4. Incline DB press specific warm-up:
   - **40s × 10**
   - **55s × 6**

## Main Workout

### 1. Incline Dumbbell Press
**Target:** 4 × 6–10 @ **RPE 7–9**
- **Set 1:** 70s × 10 (RPE 7)
- **Set 2:** 75s × 9 (RPE 7.5)
- **Set 3:** 80s × 8 (RPE 8)
- **Set 4:** 80s × 7 (RPE 8.5)
**Rest:** 2–3 min between working sets  
**Notes:** 2–3 sec eccentric, slight pause near chest, drive up without bouncing. If shoulders feel cranky, tuck elbows ~30–45°.

### 2. Machine Chest Press (neutral if available)
**Target:** 3 × 8–12 @ **RPE 8**
- **Warm-up:** 140 × 10 (easy)
- **Set 1:** 180 × 12 (RPE 7.5)
- **Set 2:** 200 × 10 (RPE 8)
- **Set 3:** 200 × 9 (RPE 8.5)
**Rest:** 2 min between working sets  
**Notes:** Keep scapula retracted; stop 1–2 cm short of hard lockout to keep chest tension.

### 3. Single-Arm Cable Lateral Raise (slight lean)
**Target:** 5 × 12–20 @ **RPE 8–9**
- **Set 1:** 12.5 × 20/side (RPE 8)
- **Set 2:** 12.5 × 18/side (RPE 8.5)
- **Set 3:** 12.5 × 16/side (RPE 9)
- **Set 4:** 10 × 18/side (RPE 9)
- **Set 5:** 10 × 16/side (RPE 9)
**Rest:** 45–60 sec between sets (alternate sides)  
**Notes:** Lead with elbow; stop around shoulder height; 1-sec peak squeeze; no torso swing.

### 4. High-to-Low Cable Fly
**Target:** 3 × 12–15 @ **RPE 8**
- **Set 1:** 25 × 15 (RPE 8)
- **Set 2:** 25 × 14 (RPE 8.5)
- **Set 3:** 20 × 15 (RPE 8.5)
**Rest:** 60–75 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Hands finish low/inside; ribs down; keep shoulders out of it.

### 5. Overhead Cable Triceps Extension (rope, split stance)
**Target:** 4 × 10–15 @ **RPE 8–9**
- **Warm-up:** 40 × 12
- **Set 1:** 55 × 15 (RPE 8)
- **Set 2:** 60 × 13 (RPE 8.5)
- **Set 3:** 60 × 12 (RPE 9)
- **Set 4:** 55 × 12 (RPE 9)
**Rest:** 60–90 sec between working sets  
**Notes:** Elbows slightly in; reach long at top; keep upper arm angled forward to bias long head.

### 6. Cable Pressdown (straight bar or rope)
**Target:** 3 × 10–15 @ **RPE 8**
- **Set 1:** 70 × 15 (RPE 8)
- **Set 2:** 80 × 12 (RPE 8.5)
- **Set 3:** 70 × 12 (RPE 8.5)
**Rest:** 60–75 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Don’t flare shoulders; pause 1 sec at full extension.

### 7. Pec Deck (optional, only if chest feels fresh)
**Target:** 1–2 × 12–20 @ **RPE 8**
- **Set 1:** 140 × 15 (RPE 8)
- **Set 2:** 140 × 12 (RPE 8.5) *(optional)*
**Rest:** 60 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Controlled stretch; no ego ROM.

## Cool Down (6 minutes)
1. Doorway pec stretch: **30 sec/side**
2. Overhead triceps/lat stretch: **30 sec/side**
3. Cable or band dislocates (very light): **1 × 15**
4. Easy walk: **2 min**

## Notes
- Goal today: establish stable pressing numbers and get a big lateral delt + long-head triceps stimulus without joint irritation.
- Total time: ~90 minutes.

---

# Workout — Tuesday, February 24, 2026
**Focus:** Back density/width + high rear-delt stimulus  
**Location:** LA Fitness  
**Duration:** ~85–95 minutes

## Warm-Up (10 minutes)
1. Row erg or ski erg: **5 min** easy
2. Dead hang (or assisted): **2 × 20–30 sec**
3. Scap pull-ups: **2 × 6**
4. Straight-arm pulldown (very light): **1 × 15**

## Main Workout

### 1. Neutral-Grip Pulldown (or weighted pull-up if preferred)
**Target:** 4 × 6–10 @ **RPE 7–9**
- **Warm-up:** 120 × 10
- **Set 1:** 160 × 10 (RPE 7.5)
- **Set 2:** 170 × 9 (RPE 8)
- **Set 3:** 180 × 8 (RPE 8.5)
- **Set 4:** 170 × 8 (RPE 8.5)
**Rest:** 2–3 min between working sets  
**Notes:** Think “elbows to ribs,” 1-sec pause at chest, full stretch without losing shoulder position.

### 2. Chest-Supported Row (machine or incline bench DB row)
**Target:** 4 × 8–12 @ **RPE 8**
- **Warm-up:** 90 × 12
- **Set 1:** 140 × 12 (RPE 8)
- **Set 2:** 160 × 10 (RPE 8.5)
- **Set 3:** 160 × 9 (RPE 9)
- **Set 4:** 140 × 10 (RPE 8.5)
**Rest:** 2 min between working sets  
**Notes:** Drive elbows back; don’t shrug; keep chest glued to pad.

### 3. One-Arm Cable Lat Pull-In (lat “sweep”)
**Target:** 3 × 10–15 @ **RPE 8**
- **Set 1:** 35 × 15/side (RPE 8)
- **Set 2:** 40 × 12/side (RPE 8.5)
- **Set 3:** 40 × 11/side (RPE 9)
**Rest:** 60–75 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Slight torso lean; pull elbow toward back pocket; slow return.

### 4. Reverse Pec Deck
**Target:** 4 × 12–20 @ **RPE 8–9**
- **Set 1:** 100 × 20 (RPE 8)
- **Set 2:** 110 × 17 (RPE 8.5)
- **Set 3:** 110 × 15 (RPE 9)
- **Set 4:** 100 × 15 (RPE 9)
**Rest:** 60 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Hands “hooked,” elbows slightly bent; pull out-and-back; 1-sec peak.

### 5. Cable Rear Delt Fly (cross-body)
**Target:** 3 × 12–20 @ **RPE 8–9**
- **Set 1:** 12.5 × 20/side (RPE 8)
- **Set 2:** 12.5 × 18/side (RPE 8.5)
- **Set 3:** 12.5 × 15/side (RPE 9)
**Rest:** 45–60 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Keep shoulder down; don’t turn it into a trap move.

### 6. EZ-Bar Curl
**Target:** 3 × 8–12 @ **RPE 8**
- **Warm-up:** 50 × 10
- **Set 1:** 70 × 12 (RPE 8)
- **Set 2:** 75 × 10 (RPE 8.5)
- **Set 3:** 70 × 10 (RPE 8.5)
**Rest:** 75–90 sec between sets  
**Notes:** No hip drive; 2-sec eccentric.

### 7. Cable Hammer Curl (rope)
**Target:** 2 × 10–15 @ **RPE 8**
- **Set 1:** 50 × 15 (RPE 8)
- **Set 2:** 55 × 12 (RPE 8.5)
**Rest:** 60 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Keep elbows slightly forward; squeeze brachialis/forearm.

## Cool Down (6 minutes)
1. Lat stretch (bench prayer stretch): **45 sec**
2. Rear delt/pec stretch (cross-body): **30 sec/side**
3. Thoracic extension over foam roller: **6–8 slow reps**

## Notes
- Rear delt volume is intentionally high—keep reps clean and don’t shrug.
- Total time: ~90 minutes.

---

# Workout — Wednesday, February 25, 2026
**Focus:** Knee-smart quad bias + hamstrings (no heavy back squat)  
**Location:** LA Fitness  
**Duration:** ~95–110 minutes

## Warm-Up (12 minutes)
1. Bike: **5 min** easy
2. Knee/ankle prep:
   - Tibialis raises (bodyweight against wall): **2 × 15**
   - Standing calf rocks: **1 × 20**
3. Glute activation:
   - Banded lateral walks: **2 × 12 steps/side**
   - Bodyweight hip hinge (hands on hips): **1 × 12**
4. **Knee sleeves on** before working sets.

## Main Workout

### 1. Hack Squat (controlled depth; no forced deep ROM)
**Target:** 4 × 6–10 @ **RPE 7–9**
- **Warm-up:** 90 × 10
- **Warm-up:** 140 × 8
- **Set 1:** 190 × 10 (RPE 7.5)
- **Set 2:** 210 × 9 (RPE 8)
- **Set 3:** 230 × 8 (RPE 8.5)
- **Set 4:** 210 × 8 (RPE 8.5)
**Rest:** 3 min between working sets  
**Notes:** Feet shoulder-width, slightly low-to-mid for quad bias; stop at the deepest ROM that stays knee-friendly and stable.

### 2. Leg Press (feet mid-to-low for quads)
**Target:** 3 × 10–15 @ **RPE 8**
- **Warm-up:** 2 plates/side × 12
- **Set 1:** 3 plates/side × 15 (RPE 8)
- **Set 2:** 3 plates/side + 25/side × 12 (RPE 8.5)
- **Set 3:** 3 plates/side + 25/side × 11 (RPE 9)
**Rest:** 2–3 min between working sets  
**Notes:** Don’t bottom out; keep pelvis from curling; smooth tempo.

### 3. Seated Leg Curl
**Target:** 4 × 8–12 @ **RPE 8–9**
- **Warm-up:** 70 × 12
- **Set 1:** 100 × 12 (RPE 8)
- **Set 2:** 110 × 10 (RPE 8.5)
- **Set 3:** 110 × 9 (RPE 9)
- **Set 4:** 100 × 10 (RPE 9)
**Rest:** 90 sec between working sets  
**Notes:** 1-sec squeeze each rep; control the stretch.

### 4. Smith Machine Romanian Deadlift
**Target:** 3 × 8–12 @ **RPE 8**
- **Warm-up:** 95 × 10
- **Warm-up:** 135 × 8
- **Set 1:** 185 × 12 (RPE 8)
- **Set 2:** 205 × 10 (RPE 8.5)
- **Set 3:** 205 × 9 (RPE 9)
**Rest:** 2–3 min between working sets  
**Notes:** Soft knees, hips back, lats tight; bar tracks close to thighs; stop when hamstrings say “enough,” not when plates hit.

### 5. Leg Extension (2-sec squeeze at top)
**Target:** 3 × 12–20 @ **RPE 9**
- **Set 1:** 110 × 15 (RPE 8.5)
- **Set 2:** 110 × 13 (RPE 9)
- **Set 3:** 95 × 15 (RPE 9)
**Rest:** 60–75 sec between sets  
**Notes:** This is your high-stimulus/low-systemic finisher—make it burn, keep knee pain at **0–2/10** only.

### 6. Standing Calf Raise
**Target:** 4 × 8–12 @ **RPE 8–9**
- **Set 1:** 180 × 12 (RPE 8)
- **Set 2:** 200 × 10 (RPE 8.5)
- **Set 3:** 200 × 9 (RPE 9)
- **Set 4:** 180 × 10 (RPE 9)
**Rest:** 60–90 sec between sets  
**Notes:** 2-sec stretch at bottom, 1-sec squeeze at top.

### 7. Tibialis Raise (machine or DB)
**Target:** 2 × 15–25 @ **RPE 8**
- **Set 1:** 25 × 20 (RPE 8)
- **Set 2:** 25 × 18 (RPE 8.5)
**Rest:** 45–60 sec between sets

## Cool Down (7 minutes)
1. Quad stretch: **45 sec/side**
2. Hamstring stretch (band or bench): **45 sec/side**
3. Hip flexor stretch: **45 sec/side**
4. Easy walk: **2 min**

## Notes
- Knee rule: pain stays low and technique stays crisp. If knee feels “pinchy,” shorten ROM slightly on hack/press and keep extensions strict.
- Total time: ~105 minutes.

---

# Workout — Friday, February 27, 2026
**Focus:** Upper maintenance + arms + delt touch (keep frequency high)  
**Location:** LA Fitness  
**Duration:** ~85–95 minutes

## Warm-Up (10 minutes)
1. Incline walk: **5 min**
2. Cable face pull (light): **2 × 15**
3. Machine row warm-up: **1 × 12** easy

## Main Workout

### 1. Hammer Strength Machine Row
**Target:** 3 × 8–12 @ **RPE 8**
- **Warm-up:** 90 × 12
- **Set 1:** 140 × 12 (RPE 8)
- **Set 2:** 160 × 10 (RPE 8.5)
- **Set 3:** 160 × 9 (RPE 9)
**Rest:** 2 min between working sets  
**Notes:** Elbows 30–45°; pause on torso; don’t heave.

### 2. Incline Smith Press (or machine incline press)
**Target:** 3 × 8–12 @ **RPE 8**
- **Warm-up:** 95 × 10
- **Warm-up:** 115 × 6
- **Set 1:** 135 × 12 (RPE 8)
- **Set 2:** 145 × 10 (RPE 8.5)
- **Set 3:** 145 × 9 (RPE 9)
**Rest:** 2–3 min between working sets  
**Notes:** Lower to upper chest/clavicle line; keep wrists stacked.

### 3. Cable Lateral Raise (different angle than Monday; slightly behind body)
**Target:** 4 × 12–20 @ **RPE 8–9**
- **Set 1:** 10 × 20/side (RPE 8)
- **Set 2:** 12.5 × 16/side (RPE 8.5)
- **Set 3:** 12.5 × 14/side (RPE 9)
- **Set 4:** 10 × 15/side (RPE 9)
**Rest:** 45–60 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Keep shoulder depressed; smooth arc.

### 4. Pullover (cable or machine)
**Target:** 3 × 10–15 @ **RPE 8**
- **Set 1:** 80 × 15 (RPE 8)
- **Set 2:** 90 × 12 (RPE 8.5)
- **Set 3:** 90 × 11 (RPE 9)
**Rest:** 75–90 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Slight elbow bend; ribs down; feel lats lengthen.

### 5. Seated Overhead DB Triceps Extension
**Target:** 3 × 8–12 @ **RPE 8**
- **Warm-up:** 50 × 10 (single DB)
- **Set 1:** 70 × 12 (RPE 8)
- **Set 2:** 75 × 10 (RPE 8.5)
- **Set 3:** 75 × 9 (RPE 9)
**Rest:** 90 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Full stretch behind head; elbows in; don’t turn into a press.

### 6. Rope Pressdown
**Target:** 3 × 12–15 @ **RPE 8–9**
- **Set 1:** 60 × 15 (RPE 8)
- **Set 2:** 70 × 13 (RPE 9)
- **Set 3:** 60 × 12 (RPE 9)
**Rest:** 60 sec between sets

### 7. Incline DB Curl
**Target:** 3 × 10–15 @ **RPE 8**
- **Set 1:** 30s × 15 (RPE 8)
- **Set 2:** 35s × 12 (RPE 8.5)
- **Set 3:** 35s × 11 (RPE 9)
**Rest:** 75–90 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Keep shoulders back; let biceps stretch at bottom.

### 8. Face Pull (to forehead + external rotation)
**Target:** 2 × 15–20 @ **RPE 7–8**
- **Set 1:** 40 × 20 (RPE 7)
- **Set 2:** 45 × 17 (RPE 8)
**Rest:** 45–60 sec between sets  
**Notes:** This is shoulder insurance—stay strict.

## Cool Down (6 minutes)
1. Lat stretch: **45 sec**
2. Chest stretch: **30 sec/side**
3. Triceps stretch: **30 sec/side**
4. Easy walk: **2 min**

## Notes
- This day should feel productive but not crushing—save systemic fatigue for Saturday posterior chain.
- Total time: ~90 minutes.

---

# Workout — Saturday, February 28, 2026
**Focus:** Posterior chain + extra delts/triceps pump (joint-friendly)  
**Location:** LA Fitness  
**Duration:** ~95–110 minutes

## Warm-Up (12 minutes)
1. Bike: **5 min**
2. Hip hinge prep:
   - Bodyweight RDL: **1 × 12**
   - Glute bridge: **2 × 10 (2-sec squeeze)**
3. Hamstring activation:
   - Light seated curl: **1 × 15**
4. Lateral delt groove:
   - Very light cable laterals: **1 × 20/side**

## Main Workout

### 1. Dumbbell Romanian Deadlift
**Target:** 3 × 6–10 @ **RPE 7–9**
- **Warm-up:** 70s × 8
- **Set 1:** 90s × 10 (RPE 8)
- **Set 2:** 95s × 8 (RPE 8.5)
- **Set 3:** 95s × 7 (RPE 9)
**Rest:** 2–3 min between working sets  
**Notes:** Long hamstring stretch; keep DBs close; no bouncing.

### 2. Hip Thrust (Smith or machine)
**Target:** 3 × 8–12 @ **RPE 8**
- **Warm-up:** 135 × 10
- **Set 1:** 225 × 12 (RPE 8)
- **Set 2:** 245 × 10 (RPE 8.5)
- **Set 3:** 245 × 9 (RPE 9)
**Rest:** 2–3 min between working sets  
**Notes:** Posterior pelvic tilt at lockout; 1-sec squeeze; shins near vertical.

### 3. Seated or Lying Leg Curl
**Target:** 3 × 10–15 @ **RPE 8–9**
- **Set 1:** 90 × 15 (RPE 8)
- **Set 2:** 100 × 12 (RPE 8.5)
- **Set 3:** 100 × 11 (RPE 9)
**Rest:** 75–90 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Don’t rush eccentrics—hamstrings grow in the stretch.

### 4. Bulgarian Split Squat (controlled; avoid deep knee travel)
**Target:** 2 × 8–12/side @ **RPE 8**
- **Set 1:** 30s × 10/side (RPE 8)
- **Set 2:** 35s × 8/side (RPE 8.5)
**Rest:** 90 sec between sets (after both legs)  
**Notes:** Slight forward torso to bias glute; stop ROM before knee discomfort; stable, non-wobbly reps only.

### 5. Cable Lateral Raise (partials after full ROM)
**Target:** 3 × 15–25 @ **RPE 9**
- **Set 1:** 10 × 20/side + 8 partials (RPE 9)
- **Set 2:** 10 × 18/side + 8 partials (RPE 9)
- **Set 3:** 7.5 × 20/side + 10 partials (RPE 9)
**Rest:** 45–60 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Full ROM to shoulder height, then short partials in top half—burn, not body English.

### 6. Reverse Pec Deck (or rear delt cable fly)
**Target:** 3 × 12–20 @ **RPE 8–9**
- **Set 1:** 100 × 18 (RPE 8.5)
- **Set 2:** 110 × 15 (RPE 9)
- **Set 3:** 100 × 15 (RPE 9)
**Rest:** 60 sec between sets

### 7. Overhead Cable Triceps Extension (rope)
**Target:** 3 × 12–20 @ **RPE 8–9**
- **Set 1:** 50 × 20 (RPE 8)
- **Set 2:** 55 × 16 (RPE 8.5)
- **Set 3:** 55 × 14 (RPE 9)
**Rest:** 60–90 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Finish the week by hammering long head—clean stretch + full lockout.

## Cool Down (7 minutes)
1. Hamstring stretch: **45 sec/side**
2. Figure-4 glute stretch: **45 sec/side**
3. Quad stretch (gentle): **30 sec/side**
4. Calf stretch: **30 sec/side**

## Notes
- This should feel like a strong posterior-chain day without beating up the knee.
- Total time: ~105 minutes.

---

## Weekly Summary
- **Push (Mon):** Incline DB press topped at **80s × 8** (starter loads established); lateral delt work hit high-quality RPE 8–9 with strict form.
- **Pull (Tue):** Pulldown up to **180 × 8** with strong rear-delt volume (reverse pec deck + cable fly) to drive specialization.
- **Legs (Wed):** Knee-smart quad work anchored by hack squat up to **230 × 8**; extensions kept strict to avoid knee irritation while still getting a hard quad stimulus.
- **Upper (Fri):** Maintenance-focused pressing/rowing plus arm work; long-head triceps got another heavy-overhead exposure (DB extensions).
- **Lower+Specialization (Sat):** Posterior chain + delt/triceps pump finished the week; Bulgarians kept controlled and knee-friendly.
- **Constraint monitoring (left knee):** No back squats prescribed; ROM-controlled hack/press/BSS. If knee pain exceeds **2/10**, reduce depth and prioritize leg press + curls/extensions.
- **Weak points:** Lateral delts and rear delts were trained **3–4 exposures** with high weekly set count; long-head triceps hit **3 sessions** (overhead variations emphasized).
- **Next week:** If most last sets landed **≤RPE 8**, add load modestly (+5 lb DBs; +10–20 lb machines; +1 cable increment). If you were already at **RPE 9** early, keep loads and push **+1 rep** per set instead.</Week>

reference.response:
Upper Maintenance + Arms + Delt Touch 💪

Warm-Up:
- Incline walk: 5min
- Cable face pull (light): 2x15
- Machine row (easy): 1x12

Workout:
- Hammer Strength machine row: 3x8-12 @ 140-160 lb
- Incline Smith press: 3x8-12 @ 135-145 lb
- Cable lateral raise: 4x12-20 @ 10-12.5 lb
- Cable pullover: 3x10-15 @ 80-90 lb
- Seated OH DB triceps ext: 3x8-12 @ 70-75 lb
- Rope pressdown: 3x12-15 @ 60-70 lb
- Incline DB curl: 3x10-15 @ 30-35 lb
- Face pull (to forehead): 2x15-20 @ 40-45 lb

Notes: Productive, not crushing—leave 1–2 reps in reserve on the big moves and keep the laterals/arms strict. Save systemic fatigue for Saturday posterior chain.
